response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and systems software developer with strong web, testing, and craftsmanship credentials, but the role demands deep hardware-performance expertise — SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator architecture, GPU workload analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appears in the resume. His low-latency DLang work and C/C++ tooling experience are the closest touchpoints but fall well short of principal-level performance-architecture requirements.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services in DLang)
Broad, long tenure across web, embedded, and desktop stacks with solid engineering practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Meets minimum education bar (BS in CS) with well over 8 years of technical experience
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not performance architecture — a lateral domain jump at principal level is high-risk

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DNN performance background
